stories.title: Anthropic cuts off Fable 5 and Mythos 5 access following government order
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Anthropic has completely restricted access to its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models.
The restriction was mandated by a government order citing national security concerns.
The ban applies globally, affecting all foreign nations, including Anthropic employees.

stories.title: Anthropic shuts down Fable, Mythos models following Trump admin directive
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: Anthropic has shut down its Fable and Mythos models.
The shutdown was reportedly due to a directive from the Trump administration.
The Commerce Department expressed concern that a Fable 5 'jailbreak' posed a national security risk.

stories.title: PeopleSoft 0-day affecting hundreds of organizations steals gigabytes of data
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A critical 0-day vulnerability has been discovered in PeopleSoft software.
The vulnerability allows attackers to steal gigabytes of data from affected organizations.
PeopleSoft is software owned by Oracle, indicating a significant enterprise security risk.

stories.title: Ukraine's one-time test used fully autonomous drones to kill Russian soldiers
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Ukraine demonstrated the use of fully autonomous drones in combat against Russian soldiers.
The incident highlights the increasing integration of AI modules into Ukrainian drones and robotics.
This represents a significant, real-world application of autonomous systems in modern warfare.

stories.title: EurekAgent: Agent Environment Engineering is All You Need For Autonomous Scientific Discovery
stories.author: Juanzi Li
bullet_points: The paper introduces EurekAgent, an LLM-based agent designed to automate scientific discovery.
The core argument is that the bottleneck for autonomous scientific discovery is shifting from agent workflow design to designing the agent's execution environment.
These agents have demonstrated the ability to propose, validate, and iterate scientific solutions, sometimes surpassing human-designed methods.

stories.title: EurekAgent: Agent Environment Engineering is All You Need For Autonomous Scientific Discovery
stories.author: Juanzi Li
bullet_points: The paper introduces 'EurekAgent,' an LLM-based agent designed to automate scientific discovery.
The research suggests the current bottleneck in autonomous scientific discovery is shifting from agent workflow design to environment engineering (resources, constraints, interfaces).
These agents have demonstrated the ability to propose, validate, and iterate scientific solutions, achieving results that surpass human-designed methods.

stories.title: CRISPR tech selectively shreds cancer cells, including "undruggable" cancers
stories.author: gmays
bullet_points: CRISPR technology is being developed to selectively target and destroy cancer cells.
The treatment shows potential for tackling 'undruggable' types of cancer.
The news originates from Hacker News, suggesting high technical interest.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using the MLX engine.
The performance improvements translate to higher quality model outputs.
The updates also result in faster response times and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated support for MLX.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware for optimized performance.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, signaling a performance enhancement for local AI on Apple devices.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging apps.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing.
The core value proposition is running AI tools entirely on the user's own device for privacy/control.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, both locally and in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Agents-K1: Towards Agent-native Knowledge Orchestration
stories.author: Lei Bai
bullet_points: Agents-K1 introduces a new pipeline to address the gap in scientific knowledge orchestration within LLM-based research agents.
The system moves beyond simplistic methods (like abstracts or flat citation graphs) to capture deep scientific elements such as entities, claims, evidence, and method lineages.
This represents a step toward enabling more robust scientific reasoning by providing agents with structured, agent-native knowledge from raw documents.

stories.title: Recursive Agent Harnesses
stories.author: Vamse Kumar Subbiah
bullet_points: Recursive Language Models (RLMs) demonstrate that recursion across model calls is effective for long-context reasoning.
Production coding agents are increasingly using subagent spawning, exemplified by Anthropic's dynamic workflows.
The paper introduces the 'Recursive Agent Harness,' defining a pattern where the recursive unit is a full agent with tools (filesystem, code execution, planning), not just a model call.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon devices.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA is introducing Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents entirely on local, personal hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, leveraging local LLM execution.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental image generation capabilities.
The feature is currently available for local execution on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: My yard is dying, so I made an app for that
stories.author: Allison Johnson
bullet_points: A user demonstrated the rapid prototyping capability of an AI model (Gemini) by generating a functional app from a prompt.
The experience highlighted the current state of AI development, including both impressive functionality and notable bugs.
The story serves as a commentary on the speed and emergent capabilities of generative AI tools.

stories.title: Apple’s new AI photo editing tools mostly work, for better and worse
stories.author: Allison Johnson
bullet_points: Apple has introduced new AI photo editing tools in iOS 27.
The reviewer suggests the new features are relatively modest compared to competitors like Google Pixel.
The article implies that the current state of AI photo editing technology is not fully mature or ready for mass adoption.

stories.title: The future of Hollywood isn’t feeding prompts into vanilla gen AI models
stories.author: Charles Pulliam-Moore
bullet_points: Generative AI's potential to revolutionize filmmaking is currently overstated.
Existing AI video models struggle to produce high-quality, commercially viable entertainment content.
The current state of AI output is limited to short, unpolished bursts rather than feature-film quality.

stories.title: SpaceX is now a public company valued for its AI potential, so what comes next?
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: SpaceX's shift to a publicly valued entity signals increased investor scrutiny on profitability.
The focus is now heavily placed on monetizing SpaceX's AI and technological potential.
The article prompts discussion on the future strategic direction of the company post-IPO/valuation.

stories.title: Controversial FISA spying law expires tonight. The spying will continue.
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act (FISA) is set to expire.
Despite the expiration, the surveillance authority is maintained via a certification valid until March 2027.
The story addresses a significant, ongoing debate regarding government surveillance powers.

stories.title: $130 billion in data center projects blocked by protests so far this year
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Significant investment in data centers ($130 billion) is facing public resistance.
The protests are specifically targeting the expansion of AI data centers.
The narrative suggests that successful opposition provides a sense of 'political power' to activists.

stories.title: Learning to Reason by Analogy via Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
stories.author: Vicente Ordonez
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation of standard Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) in complex reasoning.
Conventional retrieval methods (lexical/semantic similarity) fail when reasoning requires different solution strategies for similar problems.
A new framework, Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RA-RFT), is proposed as a post-training solution to improve reasoning.

stories.title: Mana: Dexterous Manipulation of Articulated Tools
stories.author: C. Karen Liu
bullet_points: Mana is a new sim-to-real framework designed to address the complex problem of dexterous manipulation.
The research specifically tackles the challenge of using articulated tools, which is significantly more complex than manipulating rigid objects.
The framework aims to learn functional grasping and manipulation policies despite the physical complexity of articulated tools.

stories.title: SpatialClaw: Rethinking Action Interface for Agentic Spatial Reasoning
stories.author: Min-Hung Chen
bullet_points: The paper addresses the persistent challenge of spatial reasoning in Vision-Language Models (VLMs).
It focuses on how the design of the 'action interface' limits the effectiveness of tool-augmented agents.
The research investigates the relationship between interface design and an agent's capacity for open-ended spatial reasoning.

stories.title: Automated reproducibility assessments in the social and behavioral sciences using large language models
stories.author: Stefan Feuerriegel
bullet_points: The research proposes using Large Language Models (LLMs) to automate the assessment of reproducibility in social and behavioral sciences.
This addresses the current limitation where traditional reproducibility checks are resource-intensive and difficult to scale.
The study tested LLM-generated analyses against original findings using a dataset of 76 published studies.

stories.title: Before You Think: System 0, AI-Mediated Cognition and Cognitive Colonization
stories.author: Giuseppe Riva
bullet_points: The paper analyzes three frameworks (Tri-System Theory, Thinkframes, System 0) regarding AI's impact on cognition.
It posits that System 0 offers a unique theoretical perspective not fully captured by the other models.
The core concept introduced is 'cognitive colonization,' describing how AI systems influence human thought.

stories.title: SkMTEB: Slovak Massive Text Embedding Benchmark and Model Adaptation
stories.author: Viktória Ondrejová
bullet_points: Introduction of SkMTEB, the first comprehensive MTEB-style text embedding benchmark specifically for the Slovak language.
The benchmark covers 31 datasets across 7 task types, providing deep coverage for this low-resource West Slavic language.
Findings indicate that large instruction-tuned multilingual models outperform existing Slovak-specific models when applied to embedding tasks.

stories.title: Mana: Dexterous Manipulation of Articulated Tools
stories.author: C. Karen Liu
bullet_points: Addresses the complex challenge of dexterous robotics involving articulated tool manipulation.
Introduces 'Mana' (Manipulation Animator), a general sim-to-real framework for this task.
Focuses on overcoming the difficulty of learning functional grasping policies for complex, articulated tools.

stories.title: Operadic consistency: a label-free signal for compositional reasoning failures in LLMs
stories.author: Kyle Richardson
bullet_points: Introduces 'Operadic consistency' as a novel, label-free diagnostic for identifying compositional reasoning failures in Large Language Models (LLMs).
This method contrasts with existing confidence baselines (like self-consistency) by checking if the direct answer matches the answer derived from a stated decomposition of the query.
The work leverages Operad theory, a mathematical formalism for iterated substitution, to provide a new way to assess LLM robustness in complex reasoning tasks.

stories.title: SkMTEB: Slovak Massive Text Embedding Benchmark and Model Adaptation
stories.author: Viktória Ondrejová
bullet_points: Introduction of SkMTEB, the first comprehensive MTEB-style text embedding benchmark specifically for Slovak.
The benchmark covers 31 datasets across 7 task types, significantly deepening coverage for this low-resource West Slavic language.
Findings indicate that large instruction-tuned multilingual models outperform existing Slovak-specific models when applied to embedding tasks.

stories.title: The Stable Recovery Manifold: Geometric Principles Governing Recoverability in Continual Learning
stories.author: Bhavika Melwani
bullet_points: The research investigates the geometric principles behind knowledge recoverability in Continual Learning, addressing catastrophic forgetting.
It introduces 'Recovery Subspace Dimensionality' ($k_t$) as a new metric to quantify the complexity of recovering past knowledge.
The study validates its findings using Split CIFAR-100 and a sequentially trained ResNet-18 across ten tasks.

stories.title: Aerial Wildfire Suppression Planning with a Hybrid CNN-Cellular Automata Fire Model
stories.author: Anthony Wong
bullet_points: Introduces a hybrid CNN-Cellular Automata model for predicting spatially varying wildfire spread.
Develops an optimization framework to design targeted aerial suppression strategies.
Addresses operational and environmental uncertainty in wildfire intervention planning.

stories.title: Beyond Runtime Enforcement: Shield Synthesis as Defensibility Analysis for Adversarial Networks
stories.author: Sultan Almuhammadi
bullet_points: The paper reinterprets 'shielded reinforcement learning' from a runtime safety mechanism to a design-time analytical tool.
It argues that the underlying automata-theoretic machinery provides structural insights into adversarial networks rather than just action restrictions.
The methodology involves specification compilation, product game construction, and winning-region extraction for analysis.

stories.title: EvoArena: Tracking Memory Evolution for Robust LLM Agents in Dynamic Environments
stories.author: Zhiyuan Hu
bullet_points: Introduces EvoArena, a new benchmark suite designed to test LLM agents in dynamic environments.
Addresses a key limitation in current LLM evaluations: the assumption of static environments.
Focuses on the necessity for agents to continuously align knowledge and behavior with changing task conditions.

stories.title: Learning to Reason by Analogy via Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
stories.author: Vicente Ordonez
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations of standard Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) for complex reasoning tasks.
It argues that simple lexical or semantic similarity is insufficient for identifying shared reasoning patterns across different problems.
A new framework, Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RA-RFT), is proposed as a post-training solution to enhance reasoning capabilities.

stories.title: Influcoder: Distilling Decoders' Gradient Influence Rankings into an Encoder for Data Attribution
stories.author: Pascal Denis
bullet_points: The research introduces 'Influcoder,' a method to create an encoder for Data Attribution (DA).
DA methods aim to quantify how specific training data samples influence a model's output, such as identifying sources of toxic behavior in LLMs.
This work addresses the growing need to curate high-quality datasets by understanding the influence of individual training samples in large language models.

stories.title: HyperTool: Beyond Step-Wise Tool Calls for Tool-Augmented Agents
stories.author: Siheng Chen
bullet_points: Addresses the 'execution-granularity mismatch' in tool-augmented LLM agents, where atomic tool calls clutter the reasoning trace.
Introduces 'HyperTool,' a unified, executable MCP-style tool interface to improve agent interaction.
Aims to reduce context consumption and offload low-level dataflow management from the LLM's reasoning trace.

stories.title: Operadic consistency: a label-free signal for compositional reasoning failures in LLMs
stories.author: Kyle Richardson
bullet_points: Introduces 'Operadic consistency' as a novel, label-free diagnostic for identifying compositional reasoning failures in Large Language Models (LLMs).
This method tests if an LLM's direct answer matches the answer derived from composing a stated decomposition of the same query, leveraging operad theory.
It offers a complementary approach to existing confidence baselines (like self-consistency and semantic entropy) for evaluating LLM reliability during inference.

stories.title: SkMTEB: Slovak Massive Text Embedding Benchmark and Model Adaptation
stories.author: Viktória Ondrejová
bullet_points: Introduction of SkMTEB, the first comprehensive MTEB-style text embedding benchmark specifically for the Slovak language.
The benchmark covers 31 datasets across 7 task types, significantly increasing the depth of evaluation for Slovak.
Findings indicate that large instruction-tuned multilingual models outperform existing Slovak-specific models when applied to embedding tasks.

stories.title: Statement on US government directive to suspend access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5
stories.author: Dylan1312
bullet_points: The story concerns a statement from ClaudeDevs regarding a US government directive.
The directive mandates the suspension of access to specific models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5.
This indicates potential regulatory or geopolitical impact on AI model availability.

stories.title: System Prompt Leakage vs Prompt Injection in Spring Boot AI
stories.author: securitystefan
bullet_points: Compares two critical AI security vulnerabilities: System Prompt Leakage and Prompt Injection.
Focuses specifically on implementation within Spring Boot AI applications.
Provides practical guidance on how each attack functions and offers concrete mitigation strategies.
